name: Oita Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: the energy of the prefecture and the warmth of Oita's many hot springs (onsen), particularly Beppu and Yufuin
the prefectural emblem and the steam of the hot springs

description: A red field charged at the centre with a white circular prefectural emblem formed from the kana of 'Oita'.
history: Oita's prefectural symbol was adopted on 25 March 1968; the design combines the kana of 'Oita' into a circular emblem. The flag uses the symbol in white on a red field. The prefecture is famous for its hot springs at Beppu and Yufuin.

features.symbolism: the warmth of Oita's hot springs and the energy of its people
a stylised mark formed from the hiragana お (o) and the katakana イ (i) and タ (ta) of 'Oita', combined into a single circular symbol
